name: Agent: Landing Page A/B Testing
summary: A portable agent that runs landing-page testing as a continuous loop: propose, test, pick the winner, reuse what won — and ask a human at the four points where judgment beats automation.
objectives: Run landing-page testing as a loop rather than a project: every finished test proposes the next one.
Keep the human where judgment matters — the hypothesis, the creative, and the winner — and automate everything between.
Keep it portable: the testing tool, the hosting and the metrics are declared per deployment, never wired into the agent.
Reuse what wins — a message that proved itself on a page should not stay on that page.

outcomes.text: The whole loop is written down and operable by someone who did not build it: eight steps from hypothesis to reusing the winner, with the points marked where a person decides rather than the agent.
The loop was walked end to end on a sandbox run before it was pointed at anything real: every artifact produced, every human gate enforced, and not one external call made.

Meeting the real testing tool changed the design, and the corrections were written back into the agent rather than noted for later. Publishing a test by hand is now a first-class path with its own runbook, instead of the case the agent assumed away.

The winner rule now follows the statistics the testing tool actually reports, rather than the confidence threshold the plan had borrowed from a different method — a number that would have looked authoritative and meant nothing.

milestones.title: Write the eight-step loop, from hypothesis to reusing the winner
Make every environment value a per-deployment config, so the agent stays portable
Build the dashboard where a person approves the hypothesis, the creative and the winner
Walk the whole loop on a sandbox run, with no external calls
Write the runbook for publishing and reading a test by hand
Publish the operations guide, so someone who did not build the agent can run it
Get access to the analytics property that will report the results — what the project is waiting on, and the last piece before a real test can run
Run the first real test on a live page
